name: Smart Money Concepts (smartmoneyconcepts)
description: Python pip-installable library implementing ICT smart money concepts: Fair Value Gaps, Order Blocks, Break of Structure, Change of Character, Liquidity detection, Swing Highs/Lows, Sessions, and Retracements. Not a strategy itself but a complete detection toolkit for building ICT-based strategies.
entry_logic: Library-level: detects FVGs (bullish/bearish gaps between consecutive candles), Order Blocks (final accumulation/distribution candle before institutional move, returns top/bottom/volume/strength), Liquidity sweeps (clustered highs/lows that get swept), BOS/CHoCH (structure breaks and character changes). User builds entry logic on top of these signals.
exit_logic: No built-in exit logic. User must implement exits based on detected levels (e.g., exit at opposing OB, FVG fill, or structure break).
notes: PyPI package (pip install smartmoneyconcepts). Expects lowercase OHLCV DataFrame. This is the single best ICT automation resource found outside TradingView. Actively maintained. Can be directly integrated into backtrader/backtest engine.

name: Automated ICT 2022 Model (Farnam Rami)
description: Python-based rule-driven AI trading system operating within ICT 2022 framework. Detects FVGs, Inversion FVGs, and Order Blocks with automated trade execution. Focuses on London Open (3:30 AM GMT) and London Close (10:30 AM GMT) sessions.
entry_logic: Multi-timeframe structure alignment confirming FVG or OB formation. Calculates optimal entry price based on price efficiency and order flow. Requires session alignment (London Open/Close). Entry at OB retest or FVG fill with directional confirmation.
exit_logic: Stop-loss and take-profit calculated from price efficiency and order flow analysis. Trade monitored until resolution or invalidation.
notes: Medium article with conceptual architecture but no public repo. Uses smartmoneyconcepts library under the hood. Valuable for the ICT-to-automation design pattern even if code isn't fully public. The session-based approach (London Open/Close) is directly relevant to MES session modeling.

name: ICTFVG (NinjaTrader)
description: Simple implementation of ICT Fair Value Gap indicator for NinjaTrader V8. Detects bullish and bearish FVGs on chart.
entry_logic: Bullish FVG: previous candle high < next candle low (current candle bullish). Bearish FVG: previous candle low > next candle high (current candle bearish). Entry on price return to FVG zone.
exit_logic: No built-in exit logic. Indicator only.
notes: NinjaTrader-only. Useful as a reference for FVG detection logic but would need rewrite for Python. Very simple implementation - good for understanding the core math.

name: QuantConnect ORB Stocks in Play (Zarattini et al.)
description: Recreation of academic research (Zarattini, Barbon, Aziz 2024) implementing 5-minute ORB on stocks with abnormally high pre-market volume. Sharpe 2.81, 1600%+ total return (2016-2023), 36% annualized alpha. Top 20 stocks in play selected daily.
entry_logic: 5-minute opening range established at market open. Long when price breaks above range high. Short when price breaks below range low. Universe filtered to 'Stocks in Play' (abnormal volume spike from fundamental news). Stop orders placed at range high/low.
exit_logic: Stop loss at 10% of 14-day ATR from entry price. All positions not stopped out are closed at end of trading session (EOD flat).
notes: BEST ORB implementation found. Academically validated with Sharpe 2.81. The 'Stocks in Play' filter is the key edge - raw ORB without filters shows near-zero edge per QuantifiedStrategies. Directly implementable in QuantConnect Lean engine. Adaptable to futures by replacing stock universe with session-based volume filter.

name: QuantConnect Boot Camp ORB
description: Official QuantConnect boot camp lesson implementing 30-minute opening range breakout on TSLA. Uses consolidators and scheduled events. Educational implementation with clean code structure.
entry_logic: 30-minute opening range captured via consolidator. Go 100% long when close > openingBar.High. Go 100% short when close < openingBar.Low.
exit_logic: Position reversed on opposite signal. EOD close implied.
notes: Good educational reference but no filters or risk management. Needs ATR stops, volume filters, and session restrictions to be production-viable. Clean architecture pattern for QuantConnect.

name: Timely ORB on Index Futures (IEEE 2019)
description: IEEE academic paper testing Timely Opening Range Breakout (TORB) on DJIA, S&P 500, NASDAQ, HSI, and TAIEX index futures (2003-2013). Achieved 8%+ annual returns across all 5 markets, best 20.28% on TAIEX. Uses 1-minute intraday data.
entry_logic: Dynamic probing time optimized per market (shorter in US, longer in Asian markets). Entry when price breaks beyond predetermined threshold from opening range established during probing period.
exit_logic: Not fully specified in abstract. Positions held intraday with time-based exit.
notes: KEY FINDING: Probing time varies by market - US markets respond to shorter opening ranges. This validates using 5-15 min ranges for MES. The p-values < 3% across all markets provide statistical confidence. Paper available on ResearchGate for full methodology.

name: Profitable Day Trading Strategy (Zarattini, Barbon, Aziz 2024)
description: SSRN paper analyzing 5-minute ORB on 7000+ US stocks (2016-2023). Portfolio of top 20 'Stocks in Play' achieved 1600%+ total net performance, Sharpe 2.81, 36% annualized alpha. The academic foundation for QC-ORB-001.
entry_logic: 5-minute ORB with volume-based stock selection. Stocks must show abnormal pre-market activity from fundamental news. Entry on breakout above/below 5-minute range.
exit_logic: ATR-based stop loss (10% of 14-day ATR). EOD exit for all remaining positions.
notes: Strongest academic evidence for ORB profitability. The edge comes entirely from the stock selection filter, not the breakout itself. For futures adaptation: replace stock selection with session volatility filter or regime detection.

name: Dual Thrust Strategy (je-suis-tm)
description: Python implementation of Dual Thrust, an opening range breakout strategy by Michael Chalek (Universal Technical Systems). Thresholds based on previous day OHLC. Part of a 15+ strategy quant trading collection.
entry_logic: Upper/lower thresholds calculated from previous day open, close, high, low. Long when price exceeds upper threshold. Short when price drops below lower threshold. Intraday only.
exit_logic: No stop loss. Position reversed when price crosses from one threshold to the other. All positions cleared by EOD.
notes: Well-known institutional strategy. The threshold calculation from prior day OHLC is the differentiator vs. simple ORB. Reversal logic (no stops, just flip) is aggressive. Could be adapted with ATR stops for prop trading. Same repo has London Breakout.

name: London Breakout Strategy (je-suis-tm)
description: Python implementation of London Breakout, an information-arbitrage ORB strategy exploiting the last trading hour before London open (GMT 7:00-7:59 AM). Captures Asian session range and trades London session momentum.
entry_logic: Opening range established during GMT 7:00-7:59 AM (last hour before London open). Breakout entry when price exceeds the range high or low after London market opens.
exit_logic: Intraday exit. Details in code.
notes: Session-based ORB is directly relevant to MES trading. The concept of using one session's range to trade the next session's breakout maps perfectly to pre-market/RTH open strategies. Time zone arbitrage is a real edge.

name: Trade That Swing ORB (Strict Rules)
description: Fully automatable ORB strategy for NQ futures with strict rules. 170-250% annual return (long-only vs. long+short). One trade per day. Uses Edgeful for automation.
entry_logic: 5-minute opening range. Max range filter: no trade if range > 0.8% of price. No trade if 5-min candle closes below range (for longs). Entry on first 5-minute close outside the range. Day-of-week filter (exclude poor-performing days).
exit_logic: Bracket order with levels determined by the algo. Specific stop/target levels vary and must be periodically updated.
notes: IMPORTANT CAVEAT from author: 'Settings will change over time. These settings may no longer work.' This is honest - ORB parameters need regime-adaptive tuning. The 0.8% max range filter is a useful volatility gate. Day-of-week filtering is a simple but effective edge.

name: Concretum Group ORB Backtester (Polygon.io)
description: Google Colab-ready Python ORB backtester using Polygon.io minute data. Implements the Zarattini et al. methodology. Free data (2 years, 5 req/min limit).
entry_logic: Stop orders placed at 5-minute opening range high/low. Entry around close of first 5-minute candle outside range.
exit_logic: Stop loss from first 5-minute candle high/low. Take profit at 10R. ATR-based stop loss variant available.
notes: Excellent starting point for rapid prototyping. Polygon.io free tier gives 2 years of data. The 10R take-profit is unusually wide - most winners will be time-exited at EOD. Good backtesting infrastructure to adapt for futures.

name: Backtrader Pullback Window XAU/USD (4-Phase State Machine)
description: Professional Backtrader strategy for Gold using a 4-phase state machine: SCANNING -> ARMED -> WINDOW_OPEN -> ENTRY. Sharpe 0.89, PF 1.64, WR 55.43%, DD 5.81%, +44.75% over 5 years. 6-layer entry validation.
entry_logic: SCANNING: monitor for valid crossovers with 6-layer check (ATR, Angle, Price, Candle Direction, EMA Ordering, Time). ARMED: wait for pullback confirmation (1-3 candles). WINDOW_OPEN: 2-sided breakout window active (1-20 bars) with success/failure boundaries. ENTRY: trade executed with ATR-based SL/TP.
exit_logic: ATR-based stop loss and take profit. Failure boundary resets to SCANNING if window expires without entry.
notes: BEST architecture pattern found. The 4-phase state machine is directly applicable to Lucid's pullback entries. 6-layer filter validation mirrors prop-safe entry validation. Author also published an overfitting case study (USDJPY version) showing intellectual honesty about backtest pitfalls.

name: VWAP-MACD-Supertrend Options Bot (Brahmastra)
description: Professional-grade intraday options trading algorithm combining Supertrend, MACD, and VWAP for high-confidence entries. Live-trades via Upstox API (Indian markets).
entry_logic: Triple confirmation: Supertrend direction + MACD crossover + VWAP position (price above/below). All three must align for entry.
exit_logic: Supertrend flip or opposing MACD crossover.
notes: The triple-indicator confirmation pattern (trend + momentum + VWAP) is directly applicable. Supertrend is essentially an ATR-based trend filter. Replace Upstox with IB/Tradovate for US futures.

name: Freqtrade VWAP Strategy (PeetCrypto)
description: Freqtrade VWAP strategy using VWAP with standard deviation bands. Crypto-focused but logic is asset-agnostic.
entry_logic: VWAP calculated with rolling window. Lower/upper bands at N standard deviations. Buy when close < lower band with additional CTI < -0.8 filter and open price max percentage change > 4%.
exit_logic: Sell when price returns above VWAP or hits upper band.
notes: Freqtrade's qtpylib.rolling_vwap() function provides clean VWAP computation. The CTI (Commodity Trend Index) filter and percentage change filter add useful noise reduction. Framework is crypto-only but logic ports to any market.

name: VWAP StdDev Mean Reversion (FMZ)
description: VWAP standard deviation channel mean reversion strategy. Enters counter-trend positions when price breaks through 2-sigma bands, exits when price reverts to VWAP.
entry_logic: Long when price drops below VWAP - 2*StdDev. Short when price rises above VWAP + 2*StdDev. Uses 60-period WMA as VWAP proxy.
exit_logic: Exit when price reverts to VWAP baseline. 0.6% fixed stop loss for risk management.
notes: Clean, fully mechanical rules. The 2-sigma entry with VWAP-reversion exit is the textbook mean reversion setup. The 0.6% hard stop prevents catastrophic trend-against losses. Directly automatable.

name: VWAP Deviation + OBV-RSI Mean Reversion (FMZ)
description: Combined mean reversion using VWAP deviation bands AND OBV-RSI for dual confirmation. Monitors price deviation from VWAP while checking volume-momentum via OBV-RSI overbought/oversold.
entry_logic: Long: OBV-RSI <= 30 (oversold) AND price below lower VWAP band (2 StdDev). Short: OBV-RSI >= 70 (overbought) AND price above upper VWAP band. Dual confirmation required.
exit_logic: Exit when price reverts to VWAP baseline. 0.6% stop loss.
notes: BEST mean reversion setup found. Dual confirmation (price + volume-momentum) dramatically reduces false signals. OBV-RSI adds volume context that pure VWAP strategies lack. The 14-period RSI applied to OBV is novel and smart - it catches exhaustion in buying/selling pressure.

name: Mean Reversion Strategies (Ernest Chan methods)
description: Implementation of mean reversion strategies from Ernest Chan's 'Algorithmic Trading' book. Uses Backtrader. Focuses on stationarity testing and half-life calibration for optimal standard deviation windows.
entry_logic: Entry when price deviates beyond threshold standard deviations from mean. Entry threshold: +/- 1.8 StdDev suggested. Standard deviation window calibrated to k * half-life (e.g., if half-life = 20 days, window = 60-80 days).
exit_logic: Exit at mean reversion (0 StdDev) or at opposing threshold (-1.2 StdDev for long exits). Asymmetric entry/exit thresholds.
notes: Academically grounded approach. The half-life calibration for window selection is the key insight - it prevents using arbitrary lookback periods. Asymmetric entry (1.8) vs. exit (-1.2) thresholds are worth testing. Stationarity testing (ADF test) provides statistical validation before trading.

name: Futures Mean Reversion Backtest Framework (MHassangit)
description: Complete framework for backtesting futures mean reversion strategies on interest rate futures. Includes data processing, signal generation, position sizing, performance analysis. Uses z-score and t-score entry/exit thresholds.
entry_logic: Z-score based entries: long when z-score drops below negative threshold, short when z-score exceeds positive threshold. T-score alternative for statistical significance filtering. Max position limits enforced.
exit_logic: Exit when z-score reverts toward zero or hits opposing threshold. Position sizing based on AUM, margin requirements, and tick sizes.
notes: BEST futures-specific mean reversion framework found. Includes realistic constraints: margin requirements, transaction costs, position limits. The z-score/t-score dual approach is robust. Performance metrics include win rate, profit factor, rolling Sharpe, max drawdown. Directly applicable to MES.

name: QuantConnect Enhanced Mean Reversion
description: Enhanced short-term mean reversion algorithm on QuantConnect. Uses 30-day moving average with 1 standard deviation threshold. Stocks below 1 StdDev of 30-day mean expected to revert with ~85% probability (under stationarity assumption).
entry_logic: Buy when price falls below 1 standard deviation of 30-day SMA. Universe selection based on liquid equities.
exit_logic: Sell when price reverts to or above the 30-day SMA.
notes: Simple but statistically grounded. The 85% reversion probability claim assumes stationarity - which holds better for intraday than daily on index futures. Could be adapted with VWAP replacing SMA for intraday use.

name: Mean Reversion Strategies on QuantConnect (SSRN Paper)
description: Academic paper on designing and developing mean reversion strategies on QuantConnect platform. Compares MACD, RSI, and Bollinger Band implementations with stop-loss impact analysis.
entry_logic: Multiple approaches tested: RSI oversold/overbought, Bollinger Band touches, MACD crossovers. Each with and without stop-loss.
exit_logic: Varies by indicator. Bollinger: exit at middle band. RSI: exit at neutral zone. MACD: exit on opposing crossover.
notes: Useful for comparing mean reversion indicator effectiveness. The stop-loss impact analysis is particularly valuable - shows which setups are improved vs. hurt by stops.

name: Pysystemtrade Breakout Rules (Rob Carver)
description: Production-grade systematic futures trading engine. Includes breakout trading rules with multiple lookback windows (10-320 bars). Live-trades via Interactive Brokers. Author trades this system 20hrs/day, 5 days/week.
entry_logic: Rolling window breakout: long when price exceeds N-bar high, short when price drops below N-bar low. Signal smoothed with lookback = window/4. Multiple windows combined (breakout80, breakout160, breakout320 all show Sharpe ~0.77-0.79).
exit_logic: Opposite breakout signal reverses position. Continuous in-market system (always long or short). Position sizing based on volatility targeting.
notes: GOLD STANDARD for systematic futures trading. Rob Carver is a former AHL portfolio manager. The multi-window approach (combining breakout80+160+320) adds diversification and reduces overfitting. IB integration is production-tested. Not intraday though - daily/weekly timeframes. The volatility-based position sizing is best-in-class.

name: PyTrendFollow
description: Systematic futures trend following system similar to managed futures hedge funds. ~20% annual return at 25% volatility. IB integration for live trading. Quandl + IB data sources.
entry_logic: Trend following rules (breakout/momentum based). Automatic futures contract rolling.
exit_logic: Trend reversal signals. Continuous positioning.
notes: Good infrastructure (auto contract rolling, IB integration, Jupyter R&D environment) but less documented strategy rules than pysystemtrade. Better as an infrastructure reference than a strategy source.

name: AI-Trader (20+ Backtrader Strategies)
description: Backtrader-powered backtesting framework with 20+ built-in strategies, multi-market support, CLI tools, and MCP server for LLM integration. YAML-driven configuration for reproducible backtests.
entry_logic: Varies by strategy. Includes CrossSMA, and 20+ others spanning classic indicators to adaptive models.
exit_logic: Strategy-dependent.
notes: Most useful as a FRAMEWORK reference rather than individual strategies. The YAML-driven config, CLI interface, and MCP server architecture are excellent patterns for algo-lab. BaseStrategy inheritance pattern is clean.

name: QuantifiedStrategies ORB Analysis
description: Comprehensive backtest analysis of ORB on S&P 500. KEY FINDING: Raw ORB without filters shows near-zero edge (best: 0.04% avg gain per trade). Adding daily-bar filters dramatically improves results.
entry_logic: Buy breakout above highest price from 0830-0900. Enter any time after 0900. Multiple range durations tested.
exit_logic: Sell at daily session close.
notes: CRITICAL FINDING: Raw ORB is dead. The edge only exists WITH filters. This validates that Zarattini's 'Stocks in Play' filter and the Trade That Swing volatility gate are not optional - they ARE the strategy. For MES: must add regime/volatility/volume filters to any ORB implementation.

name: EMA-VWAP Automated Scalping (PickMyTrade/Tradovate)
description: Automated VWAP scalping strategy connected to Tradovate. Combines VWAP with EMA stack for directional bias.
entry_logic: Bullish: Price > VWAP, 9 EMA > 20 EMA > 50 EMA, RSI > 45. Long entry: 9 EMA crosses above VWAP with all EMAs stacked bullishly, RSI crosses 50 upward. Enter on next candle close.
exit_logic: Trailing stop based on 1.5x ATR.
notes: Directly relevant to prop trading on Tradovate. The EMA stack confirmation is a solid trend filter. 1.5x ATR trailing stop is conservative but prop-safe. Could integrate with algo-lab's prop controller.
